post.description:
Create Target

 Create a new target to your endpoint, which can be used in executions.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.write`

get.description:
Get Target

 Returns the target identified by the requested ID.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.read`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the target to retrieve.

schema.title: id
schema.description: The unique identifier of the target to retrieve.

post.description:
Update Target

 Update an existing target.
 To generate a new signing key set the optional expirationSigningKey.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.write`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the target to update.

schema.title: id
schema.description: The unique identifier of the target to update.

restAsync.title: rest_async
restAsync.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The call is sent asynchronously and Zitadel does not wait for the response.
 The response of the target is ignored, no status or body is checked.
 This is typically used for executions of type "events".

restCall.title: rest_call
restCall.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The response of the target will be checked for the status code and the body.
 In case of an error status code (non 2xx) and interrupt_on_error is set to true,
 the execution will be aborted and no further targets will be called.
 In case of a successful status code (2xx) the body will be parsed and mapped.
 This allows to modify the payload of request and response executions.

restWebhook.title: rest_webhook
restWebhook.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The response of the target will only be checked for the status code.
 The returned body will be ignored.
 In case of an error status code (non 2xx) and interrupt_on_error is set to true,
 the execution will be aborted and no further targets will be called.

name.title: name
name.description:
Optionally, update the name of the target.
 If not set, the name will not be changed.

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description:
Timeout defines the duration until Zitadel cancels the execution.
 If the target doesn't respond before this timeout expires, then the connection is closed and the action fails.
 Depending on the target type and possible setting on `interrupt_on_error` following targets will not be called.
 In case of a `rest_async` target only this specific target will fail, without any influence on other targets of the same execution.
 The maximum timeout is 270 seconds or 4.5 minutes.
 If not set, the timeout will not be changed.

endpoint.title: endpoint
endpoint.description:
The new URL of the endpoint to call.
 If not set, the endpoint will not be changed.

expirationSigningKey.title: expiration_signing_key
expirationSigningKey.description:
Regenerate the key used for signing and checking the payload sent to the target.
 Set the graceful period for the existing key. During that time, the previous
 signing key and the new one will be used to sign the request to allow you a smooth
 transition onf your API.

 Note that we currently only allow an immediate rotation ("0s") and will support
 longer expirations in the future.

payloadType.title: payload_type
payloadType.description:
Payload type defines how the payload is formatted and secured.
 The default is `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JSON`, which sends the payload as JSON in the body of the request.
 For integrity and authenticity a signature is included in the header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`.
 You can also choose to send the payload as a JWT (`PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWT`), which sends
 the payload as a signed JWT in the body of the request. This allows the receiver to verify
 the authenticity and integrity of the payload using the signing key.
 If you need encryption as well, you can choose `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`, which sends the payload
 as an encrypted JWT in the body of the request. You can provide your own public key for encryption.
 If unspecified, the payload type will not be changed.

delete.description:
Delete Target

 Delete an existing target. This will remove it from any configured execution as well.
 In case the target is not found, the request will return a successful response as
 the desired state is already achieved.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.delete`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the target to delete.

schema.title: id
schema.description: The unique identifier of the target to delete.

post.description:
List targets

 List all matching targets. By default all targets of the instance are returned.
 Make sure to include a limit and sorting for pagination.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.read`

Add Public Key

 Adds a public key to the target for payload encryption.
 The public key is used to encrypt the payload sent to the target when the payload type is set to `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`.
 The public key must be in PEM format and be either an RSA or an EC key.
 On a successful addition, a key ID is returned which can not only be used to manage the key (activate, remove),
 but also will be used as the `kid` header in the JWE token sent to the target to indicate which key was used for encryption.
 Note that newly added keys are inactive by default. You must activate the key to use it for payload encryption.
 Providing an optional expiration date allows you to set a validity period for the key.
 After the expiration date, the key will be automatically deactivated and no longer used for payload encryption.
 Be sure to activate a new key before the current active key expires to avoid interruptions in your target executions.
 You can have multiple inactive keys for rotation purposes, but only one active key at a time.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.write`

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the target to add the public key to.

schema.title: target_id
schema.description: The unique identifier of the target to add the public key to.

publicKey.title: public_key
publicKey.description: The public key in PEM format. It must be either an RSA or an EC public key.

expirationDate.title: expiration_date
expirationDate.description:
Optional expiration date of the public key.
 After the expiration date, the key will be automatically deactivated and no longer used for payload encryption.
 Be sure to activate a new key before the current active key expires to avoid interruptions.
 If not set, the key will not expire.

post.description:
Activate Public Key

 Activates the public key for payload encryption.
 The public key is used to encrypt the payload sent to the target when the payload type is set to `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`.
 Activating a new key will deactivate the current active key. Only one key can be active at a time.
 The active key is indicated in the `kid` header in the JWE token sent to the target.
 Activating a key that is already active is a no-op.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.write`

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to activate the public key for.

schema.title: target_id
schema.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to activate the public key for.

parameters.name: key_id
parameters.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to activate.

schema.title: key_id
schema.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to activate.

post.description:
Deactivate Public Key

 Deactivates the public key for payload encryption.
 The public key will no longer be used to encrypt payloads sent to the target.
 Be aware that deactivating the active key will leave the target without an active key.
 Subsequent calls to the target with payload type `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE` will fail until a new key is activated.
 This endpoint can be used in break glass scenarios to quickly disable a compromised key.
 Deactivating a key that is already inactive is a no-op.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.write`

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to deactivate the public key for.

schema.title: target_id
schema.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to deactivate the public key for.

parameters.name: key_id
parameters.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to deactivate.

schema.title: key_id
schema.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to deactivate.

delete.description:
Remove Public Key

 Removes the public key from the target. This is a permanent action and can not be undone.
 Note that you can only remove inactive keys. Attempting to remove an active key will result in an error.
 For break glass scenarios, deactivate the key first and then remove it.
 Removing a non-existing key is a no-op.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.write`

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to remove the public key from.

schema.title: target_id
schema.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to remove the public key from.

parameters.name: key_id
parameters.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to remove.

schema.title: key_id
schema.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to remove.

post.description:
List Public Keys

 Lists all public keys of a target.
 The response includes which key is active and the key's expiration dates.
 This allows you to manage key rotations and ensure that your target always has an active key for payload encryption.

 Required permission:
   - `action.target.read`

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to list the public keys for.

schema.title: target_id
schema.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to list the public keys for.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
The field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Define the criteria to query for.

put.description:
Set Execution

 Sets an execution to call a target or include the targets of another execution.
 Setting an empty list of targets will remove all targets from the execution, making it a noop.

 Required permission:
   - `action.execution.write`

post.description:
List Executions

 List all matching executions. By default all executions of the instance are returned that have at least one execution target.
 Make sure to include a limit and sorting for pagination.

 Required permission:
   - `action.execution.read`

get.description:
List Execution Functions

 List all available functions which can be used as condition for executions.

List Execution Methods

 List all available methods which can be used as condition for executions.

List Execution Services

 List all available services which can be used as condition for executions.

targetId.title: target_id
targetId.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to activate the public key for.

keyId.title: key_id
keyId.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to activate.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the public key's activation.

targetId.title: target_id
targetId.description: The unique identifier of the target to add the public key to.

publicKey.title: public_key
publicKey.description: The public key in PEM format. It must be either an RSA or an EC public key.

expirationDate.title: expiration_date
expirationDate.description:
Optional expiration date of the public key.
 After the expiration date, the key will be automatically deactivated and no longer used for payload encryption.
 Be sure to activate a new key before the current active key expires to avoid interruptions.
 If not set, the key will not expire.

keyId.title: key_id
keyId.description:
The KeyID is the unique identifier of the newly added public key.
 The ID is also used a `kid` in the JWT header for payload encryption.
 This allows the target to identify the correct key to use for decryption.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate represents the timestamp of the public key addition.

event.title: event
event.description: Condition-type to execute if an event is created in the system.

function.title: function
function.description: Condition-type to execute if function is used, replaces actions v1.

request.title: request
request.description: Condition-type to execute if a request on the defined API point happens.

response.title: response
response.description: Condition-type to execute on response if a request on the defined API point happens.

restAsync.title: rest_async
restAsync.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The call is sent asynchronously and Zitadel does not wait for the response.
 The response of the target is ignored, no status or body is checked.
 This is typically used for executions of type "events".

restCall.title: rest_call
restCall.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The response of the target will be checked for the status code and the body.
 In case of an error status code (non 2xx) and interrupt_on_error is set to true,
 the execution will be aborted and no further targets will be called.
 In case of a successful status code (2xx) the body will be parsed and mapped.
 This allows to modify the payload of request and response executions.

restWebhook.title: rest_webhook
restWebhook.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The response of the target will only be checked for the status code.
 The returned body will be ignored.
 In case of an error status code (non 2xx) and interrupt_on_error is set to true,
 the execution will be aborted and no further targets will be called.

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description:
Timeout defines the duration until Zitadel cancels the execution.
 If the target doesn't respond before this timeout expires, then the connection is closed and the action fails.
 Depending on the target type and possible setting on `interrupt_on_error` following targets will not be called.
 In case of a `rest_async` target only this specific target will fail, without any influence on other targets of the same execution.
 The maximum timeout is 270 seconds or 4.5 minutes.

endpoint.title: endpoint
endpoint.description: The URL of the endpoint to call.

payloadType.title: payload_type
payloadType.description:
Payload type defines how the payload is formatted and secured.
 The default is `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JSON`, which sends the payload as JSON in the body of the request.
 For integrity and authenticity a signature is included in the header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`.
 You can also choose to send the payload as a JWT (`PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWT`), which sends
 the payload as a signed JWT in the body of the request. This allows the receiver to verify
 the authenticity and integrity of the payload using the signing key.
 If you need encryption as well, you can choose `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`, which sends the payload
 as an encrypted JWT in the body of the request. You can provide your own public key for encryption.

id.title: id
id.description: The unique identifier of the newly created target.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: The timestamp of the target creation.

signingKey.title: signing_key
signingKey.description:
Key used to sign and check payload sent to the target.
 The key can be used to verify the integrity and authenticity of the request
 on the receiver side. The key should be treated as a secret and only known to Zitadel and the receiver.
 The signature is included in the request header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`
 and calculated over the raw body of the request using HMAC with SHA256.

targetId.title: target_id
targetId.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to deactivate the public key for.

keyId.title: key_id
keyId.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to deactivate.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp of the public key's deactivation.

id.title: id
id.description: The unique identifier of the target to delete.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description:
The timestamp of the deletion of the target.
 Note that the deletion date is only guaranteed to be set if the deletion was successful during the request.
 In case the deletion occurred in a previous request, the deletion date might be empty.

all.title: all
all.description:
Define all events as condition.
 After any event is created, the execution is triggered.

event.title: event
event.description:
Define a specific event as condition.
 After this event is created, the execution is triggered.

group.title: group
group.description:
Define an event group as condition.
 After any event under this group is created, the execution is triggered.

condition.title: condition
condition.description: The condition under which the execution is triggered.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: The timestamp of the execution creation.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: The timestamp of the last change to the execution.

targets.title: targets
targets.description:
Ordered list of targets called during the execution.
 The order of the targets in this list defines the order of execution.
 If one of the targets fails, depending on the target's type and settings,
 the execution might be interrupted and the following targets will not be called.

executionTypeFilter.title: execution_type_filter
executionTypeFilter.description: Filter for executions of a certain type.

inConditionsFilter.title: in_conditions_filter
inConditionsFilter.description: Filter for executions that are executed when certain conditions are met.

targetFilter.title: target_filter
targetFilter.description: Filter for executions that include a specific target.

executionType.title: execution_type
executionType.description: Defines the type to query for.

id.title: id
id.description: The unique identifier of the target to retrieve.

conditions.title: conditions
conditions.description: Defines the conditions to query for.

targetIds.title: target_ids
targetIds.description: Defines the ids to query for.

functions.title: functions
functions.description: All available functions to use in conditions.

methods.title: methods
methods.description: All available methods to use in conditions.

services.title: services
services.description: All available services to use in conditions.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
The field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Define the criteria to query for.

executions.title: executions
executions.description: List of all executions matching the query.

targetId.title: target_id
targetId.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to list the public keys for.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description:
The field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date.
 Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Define the criteria to query for.

publicKeys.title: public_keys
publicKeys.description: List of all public keys for the target.

sortingColumn.title: sorting_column
sortingColumn.description: The field the result is sorted by. The default is the creation date. Beware that if you change this, your result pagination might be inconsistent.

filters.title: filters
filters.description: Define the criteria to query for.

targets.title: targets
targets.description: List of all targets matching the query.

keyId.title: key_id
keyId.description:
KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key.
 It's also used as the `kid` field in the JWE header when the payload type is set to `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`.

active.title: active
active.description:
Active indicates whether the public key is active and used for payload encryption.
 Only one public key can be active at a time.

publicKey.title: public_key
publicKey.description: The public key in PEM format.

fingerprint.title: fingerprint
fingerprint.description:
Fingerprint is the fingerprint of the public key. It's prefixed with the hashing algorithm used
 and base64 encoded without padding, e.g. `SHA256:STqK+Sd4qgdu+UjiwI8NBjOD6P7UqQ42EZYIdySEyTw`.
 The fingerprint can be used to easily compare the public key with other sources.

expirationDate.title: expiration_date
expirationDate.description:
ExpirationDate is the timestamp when the public key automatically expires and
 no longer will be used for payload encryption, even when active.
 Be sure to upload and activate a new public key before the expiration date
 to avoid failed executions.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: CreationDate is the timestamp when the the public key was uploaded.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: ChangeDate is the timestamp when the public key was last changed, e.g. activated or deactivated.

activeFilter.title: active_filter
activeFilter.description: Filter the keys by their active status.

expirationDateFilter.title: expiration_date_filter
expirationDateFilter.description: Filter the keys by their expiration status.

keyIdsFilter.title: key_ids_filter
keyIdsFilter.description: Filter the keys by one or multiple ids.

interruptOnError.title: interrupt_on_error
interruptOnError.description: Define if any error stops the whole execution. By default the process continues as normal.

interruptOnError.title: interrupt_on_error
interruptOnError.description: Define if any error stops the whole execution. By default the process continues as normal.

targetId.title: target_id
targetId.description: TargetID is the unique identifier of the target to remove the public key from.

keyId.title: key_id
keyId.description: KeyID is the unique identifier of the public key to remove.

deletionDate.title: deletion_date
deletionDate.description: DeletionDate is the timestamp of the public key removal.

all.title: all
all.description:
Define all calls as condition.
 When a call to any available service happens, the execution is triggered.

method.title: method
method.description:
Define a GRPC-method as condition.
 When a request to this method happens, the execution is triggered.

service.title: service
service.description:
Define a GRPC-service as condition.
 When a request to any method of this service happens, the execution is triggered.

all.title: all
all.description:
Define all calls as condition.
 Before a response is returned to the client from any available service,
 the execution is triggered.

method.title: method
method.description:
Define a GRPC-method as condition.
 Before a response is returned to the client from this method, the execution is triggered.

service.title: service
service.description:
Define a GRPC-service as condition.
 Before a response is returned to the client from any method of this service, the execution is triggered.

condition.title: condition
condition.description: Condition defining when the execution should be used.

targets.title: targets
targets.description: Ordered list of targets called during the execution.

setDate.title: set_date
setDate.description: The timestamp of the execution set.

id.title: id
id.description: The unique identifier of the target.

creationDate.title: creation_date
creationDate.description: The timestamp of the target creation.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: The timestamp of the last change to the target.

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description:
Timeout defines the duration until Zitadel cancels the execution.
 If the target doesn't respond before this timeout expires, the the connection is closed and the action fails. Depending on the target type and possible setting on `interrupt_on_error` following targets will not be called. In case of a `rest_async` target only this specific target will fail, without any influence on other targets of the same execution.

endpoint.title: endpoint
endpoint.description: The URL that will be called in case of an execution.

signingKey.title: signing_key
signingKey.description:
The current signing key used to sign the request sent to the target.
 The key can be used to verify the integrity and authenticity of the request
 on the receiver side. The key should be treated as a secret and only known to Zitadel and the receiver.
 The signature is included in the request header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`
 and calculated over the raw body of the request using HMAC with SHA256.

payloadType.title: payload_type
payloadType.description:
Payload type defines how the payload is formatted and secured.
 The default is `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JSON`, which sends the payload as JSON in the body of the request.
 For integrity and authenticity a signature is included in the header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`.
 You can also choose to send the payload as a JWT (`PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWT`), which sends
 the payload as a signed JWT in the body of the request. This allows the receiver to verify
 the authenticity and integrity of the payload using the signing key (published on the JWKS URL).
 If you need encryption as well, you can choose `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`, which sends the payload
 as an encrypted JWT in the body of the request. You can provide your own public key for encryption.

targetId.title: target_id
targetId.description: Defines the id of the target that needs to be part of the execution.

targetName.title: target_name
targetName.description: Defines the name of the target to query for.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines which text comparison method used for the name query.

inTargetIdsFilter.title: in_target_ids_filter
inTargetIdsFilter.description: Filter for targets with specific ids.

targetNameFilter.title: target_name_filter
targetNameFilter.description: Filter for targets with a specific name.

restAsync.title: rest_async
restAsync.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The call is sent asynchronously and Zitadel does not wait for the response.
 The response of the target is ignored, no status or body is checked.
 This is typically used for executions of type "events".

restCall.title: rest_call
restCall.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The response of the target will be checked for the status code and the body.
 In case of an error status code (non 2xx) and interrupt_on_error is set to true,
 the execution will be aborted and no further targets will be called.
 In case of a successful status code (2xx) the body will be parsed and mapped.
 This allows to modify the payload of request and response executions.

restWebhook.title: rest_webhook
restWebhook.description:
The HTTP call to this target will be a POST request.
 The response of the target will only be checked for the status code.
 The returned body will be ignored.
 In case of an error status code (non 2xx) and interrupt_on_error is set to true,
 the execution will be aborted and no further targets will be called.

id.title: id
id.description: The unique identifier of the target to update.

name.title: name
name.description:
Optionally, update the name of the target.
 If not set, the name will not be changed.

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description:
Timeout defines the duration until Zitadel cancels the execution.
 If the target doesn't respond before this timeout expires, then the connection is closed and the action fails.
 Depending on the target type and possible setting on `interrupt_on_error` following targets will not be called.
 In case of a `rest_async` target only this specific target will fail, without any influence on other targets of the same execution.
 The maximum timeout is 270 seconds or 4.5 minutes.
 If not set, the timeout will not be changed.

endpoint.title: endpoint
endpoint.description:
The new URL of the endpoint to call.
 If not set, the endpoint will not be changed.

expirationSigningKey.title: expiration_signing_key
expirationSigningKey.description:
Regenerate the key used for signing and checking the payload sent to the target.
 Set the graceful period for the existing key. During that time, the previous
 signing key and the new one will be used to sign the request to allow you a smooth
 transition onf your API.

 Note that we currently only allow an immediate rotation ("0s") and will support
 longer expirations in the future.

payloadType.title: payload_type
payloadType.description:
Payload type defines how the payload is formatted and secured.
 The default is `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JSON`, which sends the payload as JSON in the body of the request.
 For integrity and authenticity a signature is included in the header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`.
 You can also choose to send the payload as a JWT (`PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWT`), which sends
 the payload as a signed JWT in the body of the request. This allows the receiver to verify
 the authenticity and integrity of the payload using the signing key.
 If you need encryption as well, you can choose `PAYLOAD_TYPE_JWE`, which sends the payload
 as an encrypted JWT in the body of the request. You can provide your own public key for encryption.
 If unspecified, the payload type will not be changed.

changeDate.title: change_date
changeDate.description: The timestamp of the change of the target.

signingKey.title: signing_key
signingKey.description:
Key used to sign and check payload sent to the target.
 The key can be used to verify the integrity and authenticity of the request
 on the receiver side. The key should be treated as a secret and only known to Zitadel and the receiver.
 The signature is included in the request header `X-ZITADEL-Signature`
 and calculated over the raw body of the request using HMAC with SHA256.
 The key is only returned if expirationSigningKey was set in the request.

ids.title: ids
ids.description: Defines the ids to query for.

offset.title: offset
offset.description: Starting point for retrieval, in combination of offset used to query a set list of objects.

limit.title: limit
limit.description:
limit is the maximum amount of objects returned. The default is set to 100
 with a maximum of 1000 in the runtime configuration.
 If the limit exceeds the maximum configured Zitadel will throw an error.
 If no limit is present the default is taken.

asc.title: asc
asc.description:
Asc is the sorting order. If true the list is sorted ascending, if false
 the list is sorted descending. The default is descending.

totalResult.title: total_result
totalResult.description: Absolute number of objects matching the query, regardless of applied limit.

appliedLimit.title: applied_limit
appliedLimit.description: Applied limit from query, defines maximum amount of objects per request, to compare if all objects are returned.

method.title: method
method.description: Defines the condition (e.g., equals, before, after) that the timestamp of the retrieved resources should match.

connect-protocol-version.title: Connect-Protocol-Version
connect-protocol-version.description: Define the version of the Connect protocol

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

google.protobuf.Any.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message.

tags.name: zitadel.action.v2.ActionService
tags.description:
Service to manage custom executions.
 The service provides methods to create, update, delete and list targets and executions.
